message:
fix(packs/package_managers): avoid false-match on hyphenated package names

Destructive subcommand tokens in pip/npm/brew/apt/yum/poetry/cargo
patterns used \b (word boundary) as the trailing anchor. Since \b
treats a hyphen as a word-break, package names like `uninstall-tool`,
`remove-cli`, `unpublish-ci`, or `yank-checker` would false-match the
destructive rule when appearing as install arguments:

  pip install uninstall-tool       -> false-matched pip-uninstall
  pip3 install uninstall-helper    -> false-matched pip-uninstall
  npm install unpublish-ci         -> false-matched npm-unpublish
  brew install remove-cli          -> false-matched brew-uninstall
  apt install remove-helper        -> false-matched apt-remove
  poetry add remove-lib            -> false-matched poetry-remove
  cargo install yank-checker       -> false-matched cargo-yank

Changed the trailing anchor to (?=\s|$) so the subcommand token must
end at whitespace or end-of-string. Actual destructive invocations
(pip uninstall X, apt remove Y, cargo yank ..., etc.) are unaffected.

Regression tests added for every false-positive shape plus four
positive-case sanity checks.
